description_long: This object is a rectangular stone block with a series of evenly spaced, parallel grooves running lengthwise across its top surface. The grooves create a striped pattern, exposing the dark interior or shadowed sections between the raised stone ridges. The stone appears to have a rough, weathered texture with a mottled brown and gray coloration, suggesting exposure to the elements. The block is solid and substantial, with sharp, clean edges and a uniform thickness. Its design is utilitarian, likely intended to serve as a barrier or obstacle in an urban environment, such as a sidewalk or park. The overall appearance is robust and functional, with no visible decorative elements or engravings.
description: A rectangular stone block with parallel grooves across the top, creating a striped, rugged appearance. The surface is mottled brown and gray, with a solid, heavy build.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full width of the block and the parallel grooves running from left to right.
description_view_1: Side-angled view highlighting the depth of the grooves and the lengthwise orientation of the stripes.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the width and the striped pattern from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side-angled view, again emphasizing the depth and spacing of the grooves along the block's length.
attribute_weathering_pattern: The surface shows a mottled, slightly weathered appearance with no significant erosion or chipping, indicating moderate exposure to outdoor conditions.
